title: University of Messina 42nd PhD Call 2026/2027 in Italy: Apply for Doctoral Scholarships and PhD Programmes Across 15 Research Fields
summary: The University of Messina (Università degli Studi di Messina) has officially opened applications for its 42nd PhD Call for the Academic Year 2026/2027 , inviting qualified applicants from Italy and around the world to apply for admission into its doctoral programmes. The competitive call provides opportunities for candidates to pursue advanced research in a wide range of academic disciplines through PhD programmes offered by one of Italy s leading public universities. The call includes positions with scholarships as well as positions without scholarships , allowing outstanding students and researchers to advance their academic careers while contributing to innovative research. Applicants interested in doctoral studies at the University of Messina must submit their online applications no later than 24 July 2026 at 23:59 (Italy, UTC+02:00 / CEST). About the University of Messina 42nd PhD Call The University of Messina has published the 42nd PhD Call by Qualifications and Examinations for admission into PhD programmes activated for the 2026/2027 Academic Year . The call was officially issued through Rectoral Decree No. 1608/2026 and later amended through Rector s Decree No. 1691/2026 , which introduced corrections and updates to the original call. The admission process is competitive and based on the evaluation of candidates qualifications and examinations according to the specific requirements of each PhD programme. Successful applicants will join a dynamic research environment dedicated to scientific innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and academic excellence. Why Apply for a PhD at the University of Messina? The University of Messina is one of Italy s historic public universities, offering doctoral programmes that prepare researchers for careers in academia, industry, healthcare, public administration, and international organizations. The PhD programmes provide candidates with opportunities to: Conduct advanced research under experienced academic supervisors. Join internationally recognized research groups. Access modern research facilities and laboratories. Develop high-level research and analytical skills. Collaborate with external funding institutions and industry partners where applicable. Build international academic networks. Enhance career opportunities in research, teaching, government, healthcare, business, and innovation. Depending on the programme, candidates may compete for funded PhD scholarships or other externally funded doctoral positions. Available PhD Programmes The 42nd PhD Call covers a broad range of disciplines through 15 doctoral programmes. Applicants may apply to programmes in: Bioengineering Applied to Medical Sciences Applied Biology and Experimental Medicine Economics, Management and Statistics Physics Engineering Psychology Chemical Sciences Cognitive Sciences Public Administrations Sciences Legal Sciences Political Sciences Humanistic Sciences Veterinary, Biotechnological and Agricultural Sciences Sustainability Sciences Translational Molecular Medicine and Surgery Each programme has its own admission requirements, evaluation criteria, documentation requirements, and available positions. Applicants should carefully review the attachment corresponding to their chosen PhD programme before applying. What Information Is Included in Each PhD Programme Attachment? The University has published 15 programme-specific attachments , each containing detailed information about the respective doctoral programme. These documents include: Programme website. Number of available PhD positions. Positions with scholarships. Positions without scholarships. External funding organizations or companies (where applicable). Admission requirements. Required supporting documents. Selection procedures. Evaluation criteria. Scoring system. Application fees. Reading the relevant attachment is essential before submitting an application. Admission Process Admission is based on qualifications and examinations. Applicants should carefully follow the University s application procedures and ensure that all required documentation is submitted before the deadline. The application process begins with submitting an online application through the University s Esse3 platform . How to Apply Applications are submitted electronically through the Esse3 online platform . The platform became available for applications starting 25 June 2026 . Applicants with SPID Candidates who possess SPID (Italy s Public Digital Identity System) credentials may log in directly to the platform. International Applicants International candidates and applicants without SPID credentials can register and create Esse3 login credentials through the University s registration portal. This allows applicants from around the world to complete the online application process. Register and Apply Here Required Documents The exact documentation depends on the PhD programme selected. Applicants should consult the relevant programme attachment to determine the required documentation. Additional forms published by the University include: Self-certification Form for Admission Qualification. Declaration of Residence/Domicile. Videoconference Request Form. Executive PhD Declaration Form. High-Level Trainee Position Form. Applicants should ensure that all required forms and supporting documents are completed accurately before submission. Selection Process Candidates will be evaluated based on the criteria established for each individual PhD programme. The selection process may include: Assessment of academic qualifications. Evaluation of submitted documentation. Oral examinations. Interviews. Other assessments specified by the programme. For each doctoral programme, the University will publish: Appointment of the Examination Commission. Oral examination calendar. Microsoft Teams interview links where applicable. Evaluation criteria. Final admission results. Applicants are encouraged to regularly monitor the University s PhD webpage for updates. Scholarships and Funded Positions Several PhD programmes include scholarship-funded positions. Depending on the programme, additional funding may also be provided through: External research institutions. Government agencies. Private companies. Industry partnerships. Applicants should consult the individual programme attachment to determine whether scholarships or funded positions are available. Application Fee Information Application fee requirements vary according to the programme. The official programme attachments provide details regarding: Application fees. Fee payment procedures. Exemptions. The University has also published a List of Countries Classified as Particularly Poor for the 2026/2027 Academic Year , whose applicants may qualify for an application fee exemption. Eligible applicants should review this document carefully before applying. Important Notice The University issued an amendment to the original PhD Call through Rector s Decree No. 1691/2026 , published on 7 July 2026 . Applicants should review both the original call and the correction notice to ensure compliance with the latest admission requirements and procedures. Support for Applicants Applicants experiencing technical issues or difficulties during the application process may contact the University s PhD Office via email. The University encourages candidates to seek assistance promptly to avoid delays before the application deadline. Important Deadline Prospective applicants should note the following important date: Online Application Deadline: 24 July 2026 at 23:59 (Italy, UTC+02:00 / CEST) Late applications are generally not accepted, so applicants are encouraged to prepare all required documentation and submit their applications well before the deadline. Why You Should Apply The University of Messina s 42nd PhD Call provides an excellent opportunity for aspiring researchers to pursue doctoral education in Italy within a supportive and internationally recognized academic environment. Whether your interests lie in medicine, engineering, sustainability, psychology, economics, law, political science, agriculture, biotechnology, physics, or the humanities, the University offers diverse research opportunities designed to prepare future academic leaders and professionals. With funded scholarship opportunities available in several programmes, applicants have the chance to conduct impactful research while advancing their careers in one of Europe s respected higher education institutions. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post University of Messina 42nd PhD Call 2026/2027 in Italy: Apply for Doctoral Scholarships and PhD Programmes Across 15 Research Fields appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FY 2026 U.S. Youth Ambassadors Program Grant: Up to $7.95 Million Available for International Youth Leadership and Cultural Exchange Projects
summary: The U.S. Department of State s Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs (ECA) has officially announced the FY 2026 Youth Ambassadors Programs funding opportunity, inviting eligible higher education institutions, nonprofit organizations, and other qualified entities to submit proposals for one of the United States flagship international youth leadership exchange initiatives. With program funding totaling $7,954,000 , this competitive grant opportunity aims to strengthen global leadership, promote mutual understanding between nations, and empower young leaders through immersive cultural exchange experiences focused on leadership development, civic engagement, community service, and the responsible use of artificial intelligence (AI). Organizations interested in implementing international exchange programs are encouraged to submit proposals before the application deadline of July 17, 2026 . About the FY 2026 Youth Ambassadors Program The Youth Ambassadors Program is administered by the Office of Citizen Exchanges, Youth Programs Division , under the Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs (ECA) of the U.S. Department of State. The program provides outstanding youth participants and adult mentors from selected countries with a unique opportunity to participate in intensive international exchange experiences that promote leadership, cross-cultural understanding, civic participation, and community engagement. Participants travel internationally for approximately three weeks , where they engage in educational activities, leadership workshops, cultural immersion, community service, and collaborative learning alongside peers from the United States and participating countries. The program seeks to develop future leaders capable of creating meaningful change within their local communities after returning home. Purpose of the Grant The primary objective of the FY 2026 Youth Ambassadors Program is to support organizations capable of implementing high-quality international exchange programs that: Strengthen youth leadership skills. Foster civic responsibility. Promote mutual understanding among cultures. Encourage international collaboration. Build lasting relationships between participants. Promote democratic values. Support community engagement. Encourage innovation through responsible AI use. Applicants are encouraged to integrate creative and transformative applications of artificial intelligence (AI) into leadership programming while maintaining a focus on ethical and responsible AI practices. Available Program Options The Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) includes eight distinct program options . Organizations should note the following: A separate proposal must be submitted for each program option. Each proposal must clearly identify the specific program option early in the Executive Summary. Applicants may apply for one or multiple program options. This structure allows organizations to tailor their proposals according to specific regional or thematic priorities. Program Structure The Youth Ambassadors Program combines international exchange with leadership development. Participants include: Youth delegates. Adult mentors (for selected programs). U.S.-based youth participants. U.S. adult mentors. Together, participants engage in collaborative learning experiences designed to strengthen leadership capacity and intercultural understanding. Each exchange typically lasts three weeks . Geographic Regions Covered The program supports participants from multiple regions around the world, including: Sub-Saharan Africa East Asia and the Pacific Europe and Eurasia Middle East and North Africa South and Central Asia Western Hemisphere United States This broad geographic reach reflects the program s commitment to building global partnerships and strengthening international cooperation among emerging youth leaders. Artificial Intelligence and Leadership A unique feature of the FY 2026 competition is its emphasis on artificial intelligence. Applicants are encouraged to incorporate AI into leadership programming by demonstrating: Innovative applications of AI. Ethical AI practices. Responsible technology use. AI-enabled problem-solving. Digital leadership. Critical thinking around emerging technologies. The goal is to prepare participants to understand both the opportunities and challenges AI presents within communities and democratic societies. Participant Outcomes Following completion of the exchange program, participants are expected to return to their home communities and apply the knowledge and skills they have gained. Participants will be encouraged to implement follow-on community projects that: Address local challenges. Promote civic engagement. Inspire youth leadership. Strengthen community development. Encourage cross-cultural collaboration. Share lessons learned during the exchange. These community projects help extend the impact of the program well beyond the exchange itself. Funding Details The FY 2026 Youth Ambassadors Program provides substantial funding to selected organizations. Funding includes: Total Program Funding: $7,954,000 Expected Number of Awards: 8 Minimum Award Amount: $300,000 Maximum Award Amount: $2,100,000 Funding is provided through a Cooperative Agreement , enabling close collaboration between the Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs and funded organizations during program implementation. Cost Sharing Requirement One notable advantage of this opportunity is that: No cost sharing or matching contribution is required. This reduces financial barriers for eligible organizations and allows greater focus on designing impactful exchange programs. Eligibility Eligible applicants include: Educational Institutions Public institutions of higher education. State institutions of higher education. Private institutions of higher education. Nonprofit Organizations Nonprofit organizations with 501(c)(3) status (non-higher education). Other Eligible Organizations Additional eligible entities should consult the official Notice of Funding Opportunity for complete eligibility requirements. Required Application Information Organizations applying for the FY 2026 Youth Ambassadors Program should carefully review the official Notice of Funding Opportunity before preparing proposals. Applicants should ensure they: Select the appropriate program option. Submit a separate proposal for each option. Clearly identify the selected option in the Executive Summary. Follow all submission requirements outlined in the NOFO. Include all required program documents and supporting information. Available Supporting Documents Applicants can access several official resources, including: FY 2026 Youth Ambassadors Programs Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO). ECA Performance Monitoring Plan (DOCX). ECA Performance Monitoring Plan (PDF). Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs Organizational Funding Opportunities. These resources provide comprehensive guidance on proposal preparation, performance monitoring, eligibility, and reporting requirements. Important Dates Applicants should note the following timeline: Funding Opportunity Posted: May 19, 2026 Application Deadline: July 17, 2026 Archive Date: August 16, 2026 Why Organizations Should Apply The FY 2026 Youth Ambassadors Program offers organizations an opportunity to contribute to one of the U.S. government s premier international exchange initiatives. By participating, successful organizations can: Expand international partnerships. Promote youth leadership development. Foster cultural understanding. Support community-based projects. Advance civic engagement. Integrate innovative AI learning into youth leadership programming. Help shape the next generation of global leaders. Apply Here With significant funding available and no cost-sharing requirement, this grant presents an exceptional opportunity for organizations committed to international education, youth development, and cultural diplomacy. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post FY 2026 U.S. Youth Ambassadors Program Grant: Up to $7.95 Million Available for International Youth Leadership and Cultural Exchange Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Canberra Fully Funded PhD Scholarships 2027 in Australia: AI & Democracy, Deliberative Democracy, and Organisational Creativity & AI Scholarships
summary: The University of Canberra is inviting outstanding domestic and international students to apply for three prestigious PhD scholarship opportunities commencing in February 2027 . Hosted by the internationally recognized Centre for Deliberative Democracy (CDD) and the Faculty of Business, Government and Law, these scholarships provide generous financial support, access to world-class researchers, and the opportunity to contribute to groundbreaking interdisciplinary research. The available scholarships focus on some of the world s most pressing challenges, including artificial intelligence, democratic participation, organisational creativity, digital politics, innovation, public policy, and technology-driven social transformation. Successful candidates will become part of a vibrant research community while undertaking original doctoral research under the supervision of leading academics at the University of Canberra. Why Choose a University of Canberra PhD Scholarship? The University of Canberra is recognized for its commitment to impactful research and innovation. These scholarships offer candidates the opportunity to conduct independent research while collaborating with internationally respected scholars and contributing to high-impact research projects. Successful scholars will benefit from: Annual stipend of AUD 40,000 , indexed each year. Scholarship duration of 3.5 years . Opportunity to undertake original doctoral research. Access to internationally recognized research centres. Collaboration with experienced supervisors and leading researchers. Participation in seminars, workshops, conferences, and collaborative publications. Exposure to interdisciplinary research environments. Additional relocation allowance for eligible international scholarship recipients (AI Democracy and Deliberative Democracy Scholarships). 1. AI Democracy PhD Scholarship The AI Democracy PhD Scholarship offers an exciting opportunity for highly motivated researchers interested in exploring the evolving relationship between artificial intelligence and democratic participation. Hosted by the University of Canberra s Centre for Deliberative Democracy, the scholarship forms part of the Australian Research Council (ARC) DECRA-funded project Smart Democracy: Fostering Democratic Agency in the Age of AI. Scholarship Overview The successful candidate will undertake an original PhD research project under the supervision of Associate Professor Hans Asenbaum . The doctoral research will contribute to understanding how artificial intelligence influences democratic participation while maintaining an independent research focus aligned with the broader DECRA project. The completed PhD may be submitted either as: A traditional doctoral monograph, or A thesis by publication. Scholarship Benefits Recipients will receive: AUD 40,000 annual stipend. Annual indexation. Scholarship duration of 3.5 years. Relocation allowance for eligible international students. Membership in one of the world s leading research centres on deliberative democracy. Opportunities to co-author publications arising from the DECRA project. Participation in collaborative research activities, literature reviews, and action research. Research Areas Applicants are encouraged to submit proposals in areas such as: Artificial Intelligence and Democracy Democratic Participation Democratic Innovations Social Movements Activism Deliberative Democracy Participatory Democracy Radical Democracy Critical Theory Phenomenology Affect Theory Feminist Theory Queer Theory Posthumanism New Materialism Assemblage Theory Qualitative Research Quantitative Research Mixed Methods Research Projects may be theoretical, empirical, or interdisciplinary. Eligibility Applicants must: Be Australian citizens, Australian permanent residents, New Zealand citizens, or international applicants eligible to study in Australia on a student visa. Hold: A Bachelor s degree with First Class or Upper Second Class Honours; or A Master s degree containing a substantial research component; or An equivalent qualification recognized by the University of Canberra. Meet the University s PhD admission requirements. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: A PhD research proposal (maximum three pages) outlining: Research topic. Relevance. Research questions. Theoretical framework. Methodology. Connection to the Smart Democracy DECRA project. Curriculum Vitae (CV). Academic transcripts. Writing sample (maximum 30 pages). Letter of recommendation submitted directly by the referee. Names and contact details of two academic referees. Important Dates Program commencement: February 2027. Application deadline: 14 August 2026 (12:00 pm AEST) . Shortlisted interviews: 1–2 September 2026. Notification of successful applicants: 21 September 2026. 2. Organisational Creativity and AI PhD Stipend Scholarship The Organisational Creativity and AI PhD Stipend Scholarship is offered by the Faculty of Business, Government and Law. The research project investigates how technology influences creativity, curiosity, and empowerment among consumers and organizations. Research Focus The project explores: Consumer technology use. Organisational technology adoption. Creativity. Curiosity. Empowerment. Business innovation. Candidates will contribute to research examining how emerging technologies affect creative thinking and organizational performance. Scholarship Benefits Successful candidates receive: AUD 40,000 annual stipend. Annual indexation. Funding for 3.5 years. Commencement in Semester 1, 2027. Eligibility Applicants must: Be Australian citizens or permanent residents. Meet University PhD admission requirements. Hold a Master of Research or First Class Honours degree in a related discipline. Meet English language requirements. Not already possess a PhD. Be able to commence in the year of offer. Enrol full-time. Application Process Interested applicants should email the CanBe Lab Director, Dr. Ryan Payne , including: Curriculum Vitae. Undergraduate and postgraduate academic results. One-page research proposal containing: Proposed thesis title. Business research problem to be investigated. Selected applicants will subsequently complete the formal University PhD application process. Deadline Initial enquiries must be submitted by 15 August 2026 . 3. Deliberative Democracy PhD Scholarship The Deliberative Democracy PhD Scholarship provides an outstanding opportunity to join the internationally respected Centre for Deliberative Democracy and conduct independent research on democratic participation and democratic innovation. Candidates will work alongside leading scholars investigating some of today s most significant democratic challenges. Scholarship Benefits Recipients receive: AUD 40,000 annual stipend. Annual indexation. International relocation allowance (eligible students). Funding for 3.5 years. Access to leading democratic research networks. Participation in seminars, workshops, and collaborative research. Research Topics Applicants may investigate areas including: Crisis of Democracy. Democratic Resilience. Deliberative Reasoning. Democratic Innovations. Deliberative Systems. Environmental Politics. Inclusion. Identity. Gender. Feminist Research. AI and Digital Politics. Nonhuman Participation. Projects may adopt: Qualitative methods. Quantitative methods. Mixed-methods. Participatory research. Interdisciplinary approaches. Supervision Successful applicants will be matched with an appropriate supervisory team. Potential supervisors include: Associate Professor Hans Asenbaum. Professor Selen Ercan. Dr Adele Webb. Professor Simon Niemeyer. Eligibility Applicants must: Be Australian citizens, Australian permanent residents, New Zealand citizens, or international applicants eligible for an Australian student visa. Hold: First Class or Upper Second Class Honours; or Master s degree with substantial research; or Equivalent qualification accepted by the University. Required Documents Applicants should prepare: Three-page PhD proposal demonstrating relevance to deliberative and participatory democracy. CV. Academic transcripts. Writing sample (maximum 30 pages). Letter of recommendation. Contact information for two referees. Important Dates Program commencement: February 2027. Application deadline: 10 August 2026 (12:00 pm AEST) . Interviews: 14–18 September 2026. Notification of successful candidates: 21 September 2026. Why Apply for These Scholarships? These scholarships provide exceptional opportunities for future researchers to: Conduct internationally significant doctoral research. Receive competitive financial support. Collaborate with leading academics. Publish research with internationally recognized scholars. Develop advanced research skills. Contribute to major interdisciplinary research initiatives. Build an academic career in Australia and beyond. Whether your interests lie in artificial intelligence, democracy, organisational behaviour, digital politics, public policy, or business innovation, these scholarships provide a platform to undertake impactful research that addresses real-world challenges. Apply Here: submit application via, BGLResearchCentres@canberra.edu.au . Key Deadlines Applicants should take note of the following deadlines: 10 August 2026 (12:00 pm AEST): Deliberative Democracy PhD Scholarship application deadline. 14 August 2026 (12:00 pm AEST): AI Democracy PhD Scholarship application deadline. 15 August 2026: Organisational Creativity and AI PhD Stipend Scholarship initial enquiry deadline. Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their research proposals, academic transcripts, writing samples, CVs, and recommendation letters well in advance to ensure a competitive application. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post University of Canberra Fully Funded PhD Scholarships 2027 in Australia: AI Democracy, Deliberative Democracy, and Organisational Creativity AI Scholarships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Study in Italy 2026–2027: ARDSU Basilicata Fully Funded Scholarships for International Master’s Students at the University of Basilicata
summary: Studying in Italy is a dream for many international students, and the ARDSU Basilicata Scholarships for Foreign Students 2026–2027 provide an outstanding opportunity to make that dream a reality. The Regional Agency for the Right to University Education of Basilicata (ARDSU Basilicata) has officially opened applications for 12 scholarships for international students wishing to pursue eligible Master s degree (MSc) programmes at the University of Basilicata (UNIBAS) during the 2026–2027 academic year. The scholarship programme is part of the University of Basilicata s internationalisation strategy and is designed to attract highly qualified students from around the world while strengthening international academic and scientific cooperation. The initiative is implemented through Memorandums of Understanding signed between ARDSU Basilicata and the participating departments of the University of Basilicata in June 2026. Unlike many scholarships that only cover tuition, the ARDSU Basilicata Scholarship provides accommodation, meals, and a one-time financial contribution to assist with travel, visa, insurance, and other essential living expenses, making it an attractive opportunity for talented international students seeking postgraduate education in Europe. About the ARDSU Basilicata Scholarship The ARDSU Basilicata Scholarship is offered by the Regional Agency for the Right to University Education of Basilicata for the 2026–2027 Academic Year . The programme supports foreign students enrolling in eligible Master of Science (MSc) programmes offered by three academic departments of the University of Basilicata. The scholarship seeks to: Attract outstanding international students. Promote academic excellence. Strengthen global research collaboration. Enhance the internationalisation of the University of Basilicata. Encourage cultural and scientific exchange between Italy and the rest of the world. Participating Departments Scholarships are available for eligible Master s programmes offered by the following departments: 1. Department of Agricultural, Forestry, Food and Environmental Sciences (DAFE-UNIBAS) Students may pursue programmes related to: Agricultural Sciences Forestry Food Science Environmental Sciences Sustainable Agriculture Natural Resources 2. Department of Basic and Applied Sciences (DiSBA-UNIBAS) Eligible fields include areas such as: Biology Biotechnology Chemistry Physics Applied Sciences Scientific Research 3. Department for Humanistic, Scientific and Social Innovation (DIUSS-UNIBAS) Students interested in interdisciplinary and social sciences may apply for programmes covering: Humanities Social Sciences Innovation Cultural Studies Social Development Public Policy Applicants should carefully consult the department-specific annexes to determine which Master s programmes are eligible under the scholarship scheme. Number of Scholarships Available ARDSU Basilicata will award: 12 scholarships reserved exclusively for foreign students enrolling in eligible Master s programmes for the 2026–2027 academic year. Scholarship Duration The scholarship is tenable for: 12 months From 1 October 2026 to 30 September 2027 Students may also apply for renewal for the following academic year, provided they meet the required academic progress, including obtaining at least 18 university credits (CFU) and maintaining full-time enrolment. What Does the Scholarship Cover? One of the strongest aspects of this programme is its comprehensive support package. Successful applicants will receive: 1. Free Accommodation Students will be accommodated free of charge in one of the ARDSU university residences located in: Potenza Matera The residences include kitchen facilities for weekend meals. 2. Free Meals Recipients will receive: Two meal vouchers per day Valid Monday to Friday Redeemable at the university canteens in Potenza or Matera campuses. 3. One-Time Financial Contribution Students will receive a one-time grant to assist with: International travel Visa costs Mandatory health insurance Regional tax Tuition fees (where applicable) Local transportation Other essential living expenses The amount depends on the distance between Italy and the applicant s country of origin: Distance from Italy One-Time Grant 500–1,499 km €1,500 1,500–2,999 km €2,000 3,000–5,999 km €2,500 6,000–8,999 km €3,500 More than 9,000 km €4,000 The grant is paid in two instalments: 50% by 31 October 2026 after enrolment verification. Remaining 50% by 31 December 2026 after verification of regular class attendance. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy several eligibility conditions. Nationality The scholarship is reserved for foreign (international) students . Academic Qualification Applicants must: Hold (or obtain by 1 August 2026 ) a Bachelor s degree relevant to the chosen MSc programme. Meet the curricular requirements specified by the selected department. Hold qualifications that permit admission into the chosen Master s programme. Age Limit Applicants must: Be 18 years or older Be no older than 26 years on the application deadline. Academic Merit Applicants must have: A GPA equivalent to at least 70% of the maximum grade available within their national grading system. Applications are evaluated using an Italian grade conversion methodology based on GPA and final Bachelor s degree results. Language Requirements Italian-Taught Programmes Applicants may provide evidence of: Italian language proficiency from A1 level (CEFR) onward. Students without Italian language skills may attend Italian language courses organised by UNIBAS. English-Taught Courses Where courses are taught in English, applicants may submit proof of: English language proficiency at B1 level (CEFR) or higher. Applicants from English-speaking countries may self-certify their English proficiency. Required Documents Applicants must submit the following (PDF format): Completed application form. Valid passport. Bachelor s degree certificate (or expected graduation declaration). Official academic transcript. Self-certification of curricular requirements. Proof of pre-enrolment through the UNIVERSITALY portal (or commitment to pre-enrol by 1 August 2026). Grading scale statement from the home university. Motivation letter. Optional documents include: Letter of recommendation. Proof of Lucanian origin (where applicable). Reserved Scholarships for Students of Lucanian Origin Up to 2 of the 12 scholarships are reserved, on a priority basis, for foreign students of Lucanian descent (up to the third degree of kinship), provided they meet all admission requirements. If these reserved places are not filled, they will be reallocated to other eligible applicants. Selection Process Applications are evaluated by departmental selection committees. The assessment considers: Compliance with eligibility requirements. GPA. Final Bachelor s degree grade. Academic merit. Where candidates have identical scores, preference is given to the younger applicant. The provisional ranking will be published on the ARDSU website, while the final ranking is expected by 1 August 2026 . Successful applicants will also receive notification by email. Tuition Fees International students benefit from special tuition arrangements based on their country of origin. Regardless of tuition fees, all scholarship holders must pay the €150 regional tax . Health Insurance All non-EU students must obtain valid health insurance covering the full scholarship period, including medical evacuation and repatriation where applicable. EU students are advised to hold a valid European Health Insurance Card (EHIC). How to Apply Applications must be submitted electronically to the designated ARDSU contact email, with copies sent to the relevant department and ARDSU secretariat, using the prescribed subject line indicated in the call. All required documents must be attached in PDF format. Applicants should also complete pre-enrolment through the UNIVERSITALY portal before the required deadline. APPLY HERE Important Dates Applications Open: Now Open Application Deadline: 21 July 2026 (12:00 PM Italian Time) Final Rankings Published: By 1 August 2026 Scholarship Starts: 1 October 2026 Scholarship Ends: 30 September 2027 Learn more HERE Why You Should Apply The ARDSU Basilicata Scholarship offers much more than financial assistance. Successful applicants will have the opportunity to: Study at a respected Italian public university. Live in Italy with accommodation and meals covered. Receive financial assistance for travel, visas, insurance, and living expenses. Join an international academic community. Gain access to European research and career opportunities. Experience Italian culture while pursuing a postgraduate degree. For ambitious international students seeking an affordable and high-quality Master s education in Europe, this scholarship represents an excellent opportunity. Visit the ARDSU Basilicata Scholarships for International Master s Students at the University of Basilicata page for further details. Conclusion The ARDSU Basilicata Scholarships for Foreign Students 2026–2027 provide comprehensive support for talented international students wishing to pursue Master s studies at the University of Basilicata . Through free accommodation, daily meals, travel assistance, and additional financial support, the programme significantly reduces the financial burden of studying abroad while promoting academic excellence and international collaboration. If you meet the eligibility requirements and aspire to study in Italy, this scholarship is an opportunity worth pursuing. Be sure to prepare all required documents carefully and submit your application before 21 July 2026 . Discover more global Fully Funded Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Study in Italy 2026–2027: ARDSU Basilicata Fully Funded Scholarships for International Master s Students at the University of Basilicata appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Childlight Global Data Fellow Programme 2027: Fully Funded International Fellowship for Child Protection Professionals
summary: Professionals dedicated to protecting children through research, evidence, and data now have an outstanding opportunity to join one of the world s leading international child protection initiatives. Childlight, the Global Child Safety Institute based at the University of Edinburgh, has officially opened applications for its prestigious Global Data Fellow (GDF) Programme 2027 . This highly respected 12-month fellowship brings together experts from around the world who are passionate about strengthening child protection systems through innovative research, collaboration, and evidence-based policymaking. The programme provides participants with an exceptional opportunity to work alongside international experts while contributing to groundbreaking research focused on preventing child sexual exploitation and abuse (CSEA). If you are a professional working in child protection, public health, social work, law enforcement, academia, technology, policy development, or related disciplines, this fellowship offers an excellent platform to expand your expertise while joining a global network committed to making children safer worldwide. What is the Childlight Global Data Fellow Programme? The Global Data Fellow Programme is an international professional development fellowship designed to develop expertise in child protection data and research. The programme focuses on transforming reliable evidence into practical solutions that strengthen child protection systems across different countries and regions. Rather than being a traditional academic programme, the fellowship combines professional learning, collaborative research, international networking, and practical application of data-driven approaches to child protection. Throughout the fellowship, participants engage in virtual workshops, collaborative projects, research activities, and an in-person residency that enables deeper engagement with experts from around the globe. The programme runs for the entire 2027 calendar year, allowing fellows to continuously build knowledge while applying new skills within their own organizations and communities. What Fellows Will Gain Selected fellows will receive access to numerous professional development opportunities, including: Monthly virtual workshops led by global experts. Research collaboration with leading child protection specialists. Participation in international knowledge-sharing sessions. Access to Childlight s exclusive research resources and datasets. Membership in a global network of researchers and practitioners. Opportunities to contribute to Childlight publications. Presentation opportunities during the annual Childlight Research Symposium. Continued engagement through the Global Data Fellows alumni network after completing the fellowship. Financial Support Although the fellowship is not a salaried position and fellows remain employed by their existing organizations, Childlight provides financial support for selected participants attending the Edinburgh residency. Support may include: International return airfare. Accommodation during the residency. Meals throughout the residential programme. Applicants requiring additional assistance may discuss their circumstances with the programme team if selected. Areas of Expertise Encouraged Childlight welcomes professionals from multiple disciplines. Experience in any of the following areas is particularly valuable: Child protection. Violence prevention. Child sexual exploitation and abuse research. Public health. Epidemiology. Population health. Data science. Policy analysis. Education. Criminal justice. Law enforcement. Healthcare. Social services. Child helplines. Government agencies. Civil society organizations. Technology and digital safety. Academic research. The programme values professionals capable of translating research findings into policies and practical interventions that improve child safety globally. Programme Activities During the fellowship, participants will: Attend monthly virtual learning sessions. Participate in collaborative research projects. Engage with international experts. Exchange knowledge across sectors. Contribute to publications and reports. Present findings during symposiums. Participate in the Edinburgh residency. Build lasting international partnerships. Participants are generally expected to dedicate approximately 2–4 hours per month , in addition to the in-person residency. Why This Fellowship Matters Child protection challenges continue to evolve alongside advances in technology and changing social environments. Governments, NGOs, researchers, and international organizations increasingly rely on high-quality evidence to design effective interventions. The Childlight Global Data Fellow Programme equips professionals with the skills needed to: Generate better evidence. Improve child safeguarding systems. Strengthen international cooperation. Develop innovative research methodologies. Influence policy decisions. Support data-informed child protection strategies. Participants also become part of a global movement dedicated to preventing child sexual exploitation and abuse through research, collaboration, and innovation. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: Researchers. University academics. Public health professionals. Child protection practitioners. Government officials. NGO professionals. Social workers. Technology professionals. Human rights advocates. Policy specialists. Law enforcement professionals. Data analysts working within child protection systems. Individuals committed to evidence-based approaches and international collaboration are strongly encouraged to apply. Application Process Applicants must complete the official online application form before the application deadline. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to participate in an informal discussion with the Global Data Fellow Programme Manager. Successful applicants will also complete organizational approvals, safeguarding requirements, and programme documentation before joining the fellowship. Click here For additional information about the programme Application Deadline 31 July 2026 . title: Fully Funded University of Bristol Norah Fry International Visiting Scholar Scheme 2026/27: Receive Up to £5,000 to Conduct Disability Research in the UK
summary: Researchers working in disability studies and related disciplines now have an exciting opportunity to collaborate with one of the United Kingdom s leading disability research centres through the Norah Fry International Visiting Scholar Scheme 2026/27 at the University of Bristol. Hosted by the internationally respected Norah Fry Centre for Disability Studies within the School for Policy Studies, this prestigious programme invites researchers from institutions outside the United Kingdom to spend up to six weeks at the University of Bristol working alongside leading academics on impactful disability research. The programme has been designed to strengthen international collaboration, encourage innovative research, and build lasting academic partnerships across the global disability studies community. Successful applicants will receive financial support of up to £5,000 to help cover travel and accommodation costs while benefiting from access to one of Europe s leading research-intensive universities. About the Norah Fry International Visiting Scholar Scheme The Norah Fry Centre for Disability Studies has built an international reputation for producing high-quality research that influences disability policy, practice, and legislation. Through this Visiting Scholar Scheme, the Centre aims to bring together talented researchers from around the world to collaborate on projects that contribute to improving the lives of disabled people. Rather than functioning as a traditional academic exchange, the programme focuses on developing meaningful research collaborations between visiting scholars and members of the Norah Fry Centre. During the six-week placement, scholars will work closely with one or more Centre researchers to achieve a clearly defined research objective. This objective may include preparing academic publications, analysing research data, developing grant proposals, or advancing other collaborative research activities. Participants will become part of a vibrant interdisciplinary research environment within the University of Bristol s School for Policy Studies, benefiting from regular academic engagement, seminars, networking opportunities, and collaboration with internationally recognised experts. Financial Support Available Successful applicants will receive financial assistance worth up to £5,000 . The funding may be used to cover: International return airfare to Bristol Accommodation during the research visit Essential research-related expenses directly connected to the approved project It is important to note that the award does not cover salaries, stipends, or daily subsistence expenses , meaning applicants should have institutional support or alternative arrangements for personal living costs during their stay. What Visiting Scholars Will Receive Selected scholars will benefit from numerous academic and professional opportunities, including: Appointment as an official Norah Fry Centre International Visiting Scholar Up to six weeks of collaborative research at the University of Bristol Financial contribution of up to £5,000 Access to University of Bristol library resources Dedicated research workspace Access to relevant university facilities Opportunities to build international research partnerships Collaboration with internationally recognised disability researchers Participation in seminars and academic discussions Opportunities to contribute to the Centre s wider research community by delivering research presentations or seminars. Research Areas of Interest The scheme welcomes applications from researchers whose work aligns with the Norah Fry Centre s research priorities. Relevant research areas include: Disability Studies Disability Policy Disability Law Health and Social Care Inclusive Education Social Policy Disability Rights Social Security Co-production Equality and Inclusion Researchers working in related interdisciplinary fields whose work contributes to disability research are also encouraged to apply. Who Can Apply? Applicants must satisfy the following eligibility requirements: Hold a PhD or have successfully completed their PhD viva before the application deadline. Be based at a university or research institution outside the United Kingdom. Demonstrate research interests that align with the Norah Fry Centre s work. Submit a well-defined research proposal that can realistically be advanced during a six-week visit. Be committed to engaging with Centre members and contributing to its academic activities. Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following documents: 1. Curriculum Vitae (CV) A CV of no more than two pages. 2. Project Outline Approximately two pages describing: Proposed research activity Research objectives Why the project would benefit from being undertaken at the Norah Fry Centre Expected collaboration with Centre members Potential contribution to the academic community, such as presenting a seminar Itemised estimated costs, including: Flights Accommodation Visa costs Other eligible research expenses. Selection Criteria Applications will be assessed based on: Alignment with the Norah Fry Centre s research priorities Quality of the proposed research Feasibility of completing meaningful progress during the six-week visit Potential for long-term collaboration Contribution to the Centre s research culture and academic community. Why You Should Apply The Norah Fry International Visiting Scholar Scheme offers much more than financial support. It provides an opportunity to become part of a globally respected research community dedicated to advancing disability studies through evidence-based research and international collaboration. Participants can expand their academic networks, develop future funding proposals, co-author publications with leading scholars, and strengthen the international impact of their research. Access to the University of Bristol s extensive academic resources and collaborative environment can also enhance future research and career opportunities. For researchers seeking to influence disability policy, improve inclusive practices, and contribute to global conversations on disability rights, this programme represents an outstanding professional development opportunity. Application Deadline Monday, 26 October 2026. How to Apply Applications should be submitted by email with all required documents before the deadline. Official Programme Page: Norah Fry International Visiting Scholar Scheme Official University Website: University of Bristol Discover more global fellowship programs on OFY here The post Fully Funded University of Bristol Norah Fry International Visiting Scholar Scheme 2026/27: Receive Up to £5,000 to Conduct Disability Research in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NYU Tandon School of Engineering Postdoctoral Associate Position 2026: Earn $75,000 Annually While Advancing Cybersecurity Research
summary: The New York University (NYU) Tandon School of Engineering has announced an exciting opportunity for early-career researchers through its Postdoctoral Associate position at the prestigious NYU Center for Cybersecurity (NYU CCS) . Based in Brooklyn, New York , this position offers outstanding researchers the opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge cybersecurity research while earning an annual salary of $75,000 . Hosted jointly by the NYU Tandon School of Engineering and the NYU School of Law, the position places successful candidates at the forefront of research focused on protecting vulnerable and under-resourced organizations from cybercrime, financial fraud, and emerging digital threats. Researchers will collaborate with internationally recognized faculty members, mentor graduate students, and help shape one of the newest and most impactful cybersecurity clinics in the United States. For researchers passionate about cybersecurity, privacy, human-centered security, or technology policy, this role provides an exceptional platform to build an internationally recognized research career while creating real-world social impact. About the NYU Center for Cybersecurity The NYU Center for Cybersecurity (NYU CCS) is one of the world s leading interdisciplinary cybersecurity research centers. It brings together more than 50 faculty experts from disciplines including computer science, engineering, law, public policy, and technology governance. The Center is jointly operated by the NYU Tandon School of Engineering and the NYU School of Law , creating a unique environment where technological innovation meets legal and policy expertise. In 2026, NYU expanded its cybersecurity efforts through the establishment of the NYU Cybersecurity Clinic , made possible through multi-year funding from Craig Newmark Philanthropies . The clinic provides free cybersecurity services to organizations that often lack sufficient cybersecurity resources, including: Nonprofit organizations Community health clinics Shelters Legal aid organizations K-12 schools Micro-businesses The clinic also collaborates closely with: NYC Cyber Command The New York State Chief Cyber Officer Federal law enforcement agencies The Consortium of Cybersecurity Clinics This collaborative environment gives postdoctoral researchers direct exposure to practical cybersecurity challenges while contributing to evidence-based research that influences both policy and practice. Position Overview The successful candidate will work under the supervision of Professor Rosanna Bellini and Professor Damon McCoy , two respected leaders in cybersecurity research. The position focuses on developing research that examines cybersecurity threats affecting organizations with limited technical resources and developing practical solutions that improve digital safety. Rather than conducting purely theoretical research, the successful candidate will engage with real-world cybersecurity cases emerging from community organizations, making this position highly impactful. Key Responsibilities The Postdoctoral Associate will play an active role in expanding the NYU Cybersecurity Clinic while contributing to the Center s growing research portfolio. Primary responsibilities include: Conducting high-quality cybersecurity research. Investigating technology risks affecting under-resourced organizations. Developing new interdisciplinary research projects. Publishing findings in peer-reviewed academic journals. Collaborating with researchers across engineering, law, and public policy. Supporting the leadership and growth of the NYU Cybersecurity Clinic. Mentoring PhD and Master s student researchers. Translating complex cybersecurity concepts into language understandable by non-technical audiences. Working alongside faculty members on externally funded research initiatives. Research Areas Applicants with expertise in the following areas are strongly encouraged to apply: Human-centered security Usable security Usable privacy Information security Cybercrime Cyberfraud Technology-facilitated abuse Security for vulnerable and at-risk users Sociotechnical systems Human-computer interaction Digital safety Privacy engineering Researchers working at the intersection of cybersecurity, law, public policy, and community engagement will find this opportunity especially attractive. Salary and Benefits The annual base salary for this position is: USD $75,000 per year The exact salary will depend on the successful applicant s qualifications and experience, in accordance with New York City s Pay Transparency Act. In addition to competitive compensation, successful candidates will benefit from: Working at one of the world s leading research universities. Access to internationally recognized cybersecurity experts. Collaboration across engineering and law faculties. Opportunities to publish high-impact research. Mentorship from renowned faculty members. Experience supervising graduate researchers. Participation in interdisciplinary research projects. Career advancement within an internationally respected research environment. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for individuals who have recently completed a PhD or are seeking additional postdoctoral research experience. Ideal applicants include researchers with backgrounds in: Computer Science Information Security Human-Computer Interaction Privacy Engineering Cybersecurity Digital Forensics Technology Policy Human-Centered Computing Applicants with experience supporting nonprofits, civil society organizations, or vulnerable populations are especially encouraged to apply. Application Requirements Applicants must submit one PDF document containing: An up-to-date Curriculum Vitae (CV), including a complete list of publications. A research statement explaining: Research interests Career goals How previous research aligns with this position. Copies of up to three relevant scientific publications . Names and contact details for three professional referees . Applications must be submitted electronically through Interfolio , with materials also directed to Professor Rosanna Bellini ( bellini@nyu.edu ). Application Timeline Applications are currently open. Review of applications will begin immediately and continue until the position has been filled. Expected Start Date: Fall 2026 Since there is no fixed application deadline , prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize their chances of consideration. Why You Should Apply The NYU Tandon Postdoctoral Associate position offers much more than a traditional research appointment. It provides the opportunity to work on cybersecurity challenges that directly affect communities while collaborating with globally recognized experts across engineering, law, and public policy. Successful applicants will gain invaluable interdisciplinary research experience, expand their publication record, mentor future cybersecurity professionals, and contribute to initiatives that improve digital resilience for organizations serving vulnerable populations. For researchers seeking a meaningful career at the intersection of technology, public service, and academic excellence, this position represents an outstanding opportunity. How to Apply Applicants should submit all required materials electronically through the official NYU Interfolio application portal. For enquiries, applicants may contact: Professor Rosanna Bellini Email: bellini@nyu.edu Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here The post NYU Tandon School of Engineering Postdoctoral Associate Position 2026: Earn $75,000 Annually While Advancing Cybersecurity Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: €2.7 Million Grant Opportunity: Alliance for Gender Equality in Europe Launches Call for Proposals to Strengthen Gender Equality Movements
summary: Grassroots organizations and gender equality movements across Europe now have an exciting opportunity to secure funding through the Alliance for Gender Equality in Europe s Strengthening Movements Fund . The Alliance has officially launched a €2.7 million Call for Proposals aimed at supporting collaborative initiatives that strengthen movements working to advance gender equality throughout Europe. At a time when gender equality organizations are facing growing challenges—including shrinking civic space, reduced funding, and coordinated anti-gender campaigns—this new funding initiative seeks to invest in long-term movement building rather than short-term projects. The fund recognizes that sustainable progress on gender equality depends on strong, connected, and resilient movements capable of working collectively across countries, identities, and sectors. About the Alliance for Gender Equality in Europe The Alliance for Gender Equality in Europe is a philanthropic initiative established in 2021 to strengthen grassroots gender equality efforts across Europe. It brings together multiple private and philanthropic foundations that pool financial resources, knowledge, and networks to maximize their impact. Rather than supporting isolated interventions, the Alliance invests in organizations and movements that address both the immediate needs of individuals and the systemic causes of inequality. Through grant-making, capacity strengthening, and awareness-raising, the Alliance seeks to create a more inclusive Europe where everyone has equal opportunities and can live free from discrimination and violence. Since its establishment, the Alliance has: Awarded millions of euros in grants. Supported dozens of organizations. Reached beneficiaries across more than 35 countries. Strengthened grassroots initiatives promoting gender equality and social justice. Why This Funding Opportunity Matters Across Europe, gender equality movements continue to make significant contributions by advocating for women s rights, LGBTQI+ rights, disability inclusion, migrant rights, and other forms of social justice. However, many organizations operate with limited resources while confronting increasingly organized opposition. According to research commissioned by the Alliance, many grassroots organizations struggle because traditional funding models are often short-term, project-focused, and insufficient for building lasting movement capacity. This new fund responds directly to those challenges by supporting initiatives that strengthen relationships, improve coordination, encourage shared learning, and build collective power among organizations working toward common goals. Purpose of the Strengthening Movements Fund The overall objective of the fund is to foster stronger collaboration and solidarity among European intersectional and grassroots gender equality movements. Successful proposals should contribute to: Building stronger movement infrastructure. Encouraging collaboration between organizations. Sharing knowledge, resources, and best practices. Creating strategic alliances across different sectors. Promoting intersectional approaches to gender equality. Increasing long-term resilience and sustainability. Strengthening collective advocacy for policy and systemic change. Rather than funding isolated projects, the programme prioritizes initiatives that create lasting benefits for entire movements. Funding Available The Alliance has allocated a total budget of €2.7 million for this call for proposals. The funding is intended to support collaborative initiatives that strengthen movements over the long term and encourage trust-based partnerships rather than short-term transactional funding. What Types of Projects Are Encouraged? The call welcomes proposals that contribute to movement strengthening through activities such as: Building partnerships and coalitions. Leadership development. Cross-border collaboration. Community organizing. Strategic planning. Knowledge sharing. Collective advocacy. Network development. Capacity strengthening. Learning exchanges. Solidarity-building initiatives. Intersectional organizing. Projects that connect gender equality movements with allied causes—including climate justice, anti-racism, disability rights, and other social justice movements—are also encouraged, recognizing that lasting change is often achieved through broad-based collaboration. Who Should Apply? The programme is designed for organizations and groups committed to advancing gender equality through collective action. Potential applicants include: Grassroots organizations. Women s rights organizations. Feminist organizations. LGBTQI+ organizations. Civil society organizations. Community networks. Coalitions and alliances. Women s funds. Consortia. Movement-building initiatives. The Alliance particularly values organizations that embrace diversity, inclusion, and intersectionality in their work. Selection Priorities Applications are expected to demonstrate: Strong collaboration among partners. Long-term vision for movement strengthening. Clear plans for collective impact. Inclusive and intersectional approaches. Sustainable outcomes beyond the funding period. Activities that strengthen trust, solidarity, and shared leadership. The Alliance emphasizes that movement building is not simply about delivering projects—it is about transforming power, building relationships, and enabling organizations to work together more effectively over time. Why Organizations Should Apply Beyond financial support, this fund offers organizations an opportunity to become part of a broader ecosystem working toward transformative change across Europe. Successful applicants will be able to: Strengthen long-term partnerships. Increase organizational resilience. Expand regional and international networks. Share innovative practices. Build stronger advocacy coalitions. Improve leadership capacity. Contribute to systemic change in gender equality. Organizations that invest in collaboration today are more likely to achieve meaningful and sustainable impact in the future. Application Deadline Deadline: 30 September 2026. Applications must be submitted before the closing date through the official application process. How to Apply Interested organizations should carefully review the eligibility requirements, funding guidelines, and application instructions before preparing their proposals. Apply here Learn more about this opportunity on official website Learn More About the Alliance here The post €2.7 Million Grant Opportunity: Alliance for Gender Equality in Europe Launches Call for Proposals to Strengthen Gender Equality Movements appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Call for Applications 2026: Fully Funded Sabbatical Senior Fellowship, Senior Fellowship, and Postdoctoral Fellowship for 2027
summary: Researchers and academics looking for fully funded fellowship opportunities now have an exciting chance to advance their research careers through the Call for Applications 2026 . Applications are currently open for three prestigious fellowship programmes scheduled for 2027 , offering scholars dedicated time to conduct research, collaborate with experts, and contribute to high-impact academic work. The fellowships include: Sabbatical Senior Fellowship 2027 Fully Funded Senior Fellowship 2027 Postdoctoral Fellowship 2027 These fellowship opportunities are designed to support outstanding researchers at different stages of their academic careers, from established senior scholars to early-career postdoctoral researchers. Fellowship Programmes Available 1. Sabbatical Senior Fellowship 2027 The Sabbatical Senior Fellowship is intended for experienced academics and researchers who wish to dedicate an extended period to research during their sabbatical leave. The fellowship provides a six-month research placement, allowing fellows to concentrate fully on scholarly work, develop new collaborations, publish research findings, and engage with leading experts in their field. Duration Six months Fellowship period: Between 1 February 2027 and 30 November 2027 This flexible schedule allows successful applicants to undertake the fellowship during the designated period while coordinating with their home institution s sabbatical timetable. 2. Fully Funded Senior Fellowship 2027 The Fully Funded Senior Fellowship is designed for accomplished researchers with significant academic or professional experience. The programme provides fellows with dedicated time to pursue advanced research projects, collaborate with international scholars, strengthen research networks, and contribute to knowledge creation within their respective disciplines. This fellowship offers an opportunity for experienced researchers to focus entirely on research without the financial burden often associated with long-term academic placements. Duration Six months Fellowship period: Between 1 February 2027 and 30 November 2027 3. Postdoctoral Fellowship 2027 The Postdoctoral Fellowship supports researchers who have recently completed their doctoral studies and are seeking to build independent research careers. The programme provides an extended research appointment, enabling fellows to deepen their expertise, expand their publication record, establish professional collaborations, and gain valuable academic experience. With an 18-month duration, this fellowship offers substantial time for meaningful research and professional development. Duration 18 months Fellowship period: 15 January 2027 to 15 July 2028 Why Apply? Participating in a competitive fellowship programme can significantly strengthen an academic career. Successful fellows often benefit from: Dedicated time to conduct high-quality research Opportunities to collaborate with leading researchers Increased academic visibility Expansion of professional networks Development of new research partnerships Enhanced publication opportunities Career advancement within academia and research institutions Access to an intellectually stimulating research environment Who Should Apply? These fellowships are suitable for: Senior academics Professors Established researchers Scholars on sabbatical leave Experienced researchers seeking funded research opportunities Postdoctoral researchers beginning the next stage of their academic careers Applicants should carefully review the eligibility requirements for the fellowship that best matches their career stage before submitting an application. Fellowship Timeline Sabbatical Senior Fellowship Duration: Six months Fellowship window: 1 February 2027 – 30 November 2027 Fully Funded Senior Fellowship Duration: Six months Fellowship window: 1 February 2027 – 30 November 2027 Postdoctoral Fellowship Duration: 18 months Fellowship period: 15 January 2027 – 15 July 2028 Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications for all three fellowship programmes is: 12 July 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute challenges. Final Thoughts The 2026 Call for Applications presents an excellent opportunity for researchers across different career stages to secure fully funded fellowships beginning in 2027. Whether you are an established academic planning a sabbatical, a senior researcher seeking dedicated research time, or a postdoctoral scholar aiming to strengthen your research portfolio, these fellowship programmes offer valuable opportunities for academic growth, collaboration, and long-term career development. Prospective applicants should begin preparing their application materials early and ensure that all required documents are submitted before the application deadline of 12 July 2026 . Here is a social media post suitable for LinkedIn, Facebook, and Instagram. title: Springboard 2026 Research Grants: Up to £10,000 for UK-France, UK-Germany, and UK-Spain Research Collaborations
summary: The British Council has officially opened applications for the Springboard 2026 Research Grants , an exciting funding opportunity designed to strengthen international research partnerships between the United Kingdom and research institutions in France, Germany, and Spain. The programme specifically supports early-career researchers by funding collaborative projects that encourage international mobility, research networking, joint publications, and the preparation of competitive international research proposals, particularly under the Horizon Europe framework. With grants of up to £10,000 , the Springboard programme offers research teams the opportunity to establish long-term collaborations while enhancing the professional development of emerging researchers across Europe. About the Springboard 2026 Research Grants The Springboard Research Grants are part of the British Council s commitment to strengthening international academic collaboration and research excellence. The programme aims to bring together research teams from the UK and one partner country—France, Germany, or Spain—to build sustainable collaborations that can evolve into larger international research projects. Rather than funding large-scale research itself, Springboard provides seed funding that helps research teams establish partnerships, exchange expertise, develop innovative ideas, and prepare competitive joint funding proposals. The programme places a strong emphasis on supporting the next generation of researchers by ensuring early-career academics play a central role in every funded collaboration. Objectives of the Programme The Springboard grants seek to: Strengthen international research collaboration. Support the career development of early-career researchers. Encourage collaborative research between UK and European institutions. Promote researcher mobility. Facilitate the preparation of competitive joint research proposals. Increase collaborative scientific publications. Foster sustainable long-term institutional partnerships. Priority Research Areas Priority will be given to collaborative projects that align with the thematic priorities of Horizon Europe . These include: Health Culture, Creativity and Inclusive Society Civil Security for Society Digital, Industry and Space Climate, Energy and Mobility Food, Bioeconomy, Natural Resources, Agriculture and Environment Projects outside these areas may receive less priority during evaluation. Grant Value Successful applicants may receive funding of up to £10,000 . This funding is intended to support collaborative activities rather than full research implementation. What the Grant Covers The Springboard grant supports several activities designed to strengthen research partnerships. 1. Springboard Team Meeting (Compulsory) Each funded project must organize a collaborative meeting involving researchers from the UK and the partner country. The meeting aims to: Initiate or strengthen collaboration. Exchange scientific ideas. Define shared research priorities. Develop plans for future joint projects. Prepare collaborative funding applications. 2. Online Communication Skills Training (Optional) Early-career researchers participating in the project may benefit from optional online communication training designed to improve: Scientific communication Presentation skills International collaboration Professional networking 3. One-Week Research Placements (Optional) Funding may also support one-week research placements in the partner institution s laboratory. These placements are available for: Up to two participating PhD students from each participating country. The placements help strengthen collaboration while giving doctoral researchers valuable international research experience. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to research teams from: Higher education institutions Not-for-profit research institutions Each proposal must include: One UK lead institution. One lead institution from France, Germany, or Spain. The proposal must be jointly prepared by all participating institutions. However, only the UK Lead Institution is permitted to submit the final application. Applications submitted by institutions outside the UK will automatically be considered ineligible. Team Composition Requirements Each application may include: Maximum of 10 participants. Minimum of 6 early-career researchers. An early-career researcher is defined as someone who obtained their PhD within the last ten years. Activities Timeline Successful projects are expected to begin from January 2027 . Applicants should therefore plan collaborative activities accordingly. Why This Grant Matters International collaboration has become increasingly important for addressing global challenges. Through this programme, research teams can: Build lasting international partnerships. Develop stronger proposals for Horizon Europe funding. Increase research visibility. Publish collaboratively. Strengthen institutional networks. Support the next generation of academic leaders. The programme also provides an excellent opportunity for universities and research institutions to expand their international engagement while increasing competitiveness in global research funding. How to Apply Applications must be submitted through the British Council s online grant platform. Before submitting an application, institutions must register on the platform. After registration, applicants should complete the application titled: Going Global Partnerships – Multi Country – Springboard 2026 Only the UK Lead Institution may initiate and submit the application. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the Application Guidance document before beginning the submission process. Application Documents Applicants can download several important documents, including: Springboard 2026 Application Guidance Reference Application Form (offline sample) Grant Agreement Template Scotland Grant Agreement Template Reviewing these documents before submission will help applicants prepare a stronger proposal. Country-Specific Information Additional guidance is available for collaborations with: France Germany Spain Applicants are encouraged to consult the relevant British Council country webpages for detailed information. Contact Information For further enquiries: UK–France Collaborations education@britishcouncil.fr UK–Germany Collaborations studyuk.germany@britishcouncil.org UK–Spain Collaborations education@britishcouncil.es Application Deadline Deadline: 30 September 2026 (16:00 BST) Late applications will not be accepted. Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparing their applications well before the closing date to allow sufficient time for institutional approvals and submission. Final Thoughts The Springboard 2026 Research Grants represent an outstanding opportunity for early-career researchers and their institutions to establish meaningful international collaborations with leading research partners across Europe. By supporting networking, mobility, proposal development, and collaborative publications, the programme helps researchers build the foundations for larger international projects while strengthening Europe s research ecosystem. Institutions interested in expanding their international partnerships and supporting emerging researchers should take advantage of this funding opportunity before the application deadline. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Springboard 2026 Research Grants: Up to £10,000 for UK-France, UK-Germany, and UK-Spain Research Collaborations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
